title: axe-core Browser Scans, Interaction States, and Manual Accessibility Evidence · Diagnostic Repair Bay
summary: A preserved TypeScript crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, Fetch, stream, Cheerio, selector, frontier, Promise, browser, axe, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first invalid transition before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in stateful automated-plus-manual accessibility review and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggin…
objectives: Run axe-core with declared version, rules, tags, inclusions, exclusions, page state, and complete result evidence.
Scan initial, navigation, dialog, form error, authenticated, loading, empty, and changed responsive states reached through user actions.
Preserve rule, impact, targets, HTML snippet digest, help, occurrence grouping, false-positive review, owner, and retest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Axe Rule Scope
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug TypeScript team handles case 1074 during a narrow-screen keyboard and screen-reader review. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove versioned axe scans across interaction states plus triage, manual coverage, and regression evidence; URL, robots, body, parser, frontier, privacy, accessibility, and report budgets stay explicit; then change one owner permission, seed, URL, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, media, or encoding decision and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Run axe-core with declared version, rules, tags, inclusions, exclusions, page state, and complete result evidence.

Inspect the Axe Rule Scope trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

lines.label: evidence

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Axe Rule Scope, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Axe Rule Scope record says stateful automated-plus-manual accessibility review looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Axe Rule Scope.

For inspect the failure record, fetch one convenient page, omit owner authorization, source and emitted revision, URL and robots policy, runtime admission, stream and Promise ownership, Cheerio and browser differences, accessibility states, changed fixtures, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as TypeScript crawler proof. This does not satisfy Axe Rule Scope for stateful automated-plus-manual accessibility review.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Accessibility Regr…
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: crawler-ts-accessibility-regression-7b89vf" that uses case 1087 to prove versioned axe scans across interaction states plus triage, manual coverage, and regression evidence. Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node APIs, open streams or sockets, resolve DNS, parse through Cheerio, launch Playwright or axe-core, access files or packages, retain credentials, leave handles, or claim native runtime, accessibility, privacy, legal, load, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Node 24.18.0, TypeScript 7.0.2 and the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility compiler API, Cheerio 1.2.0, robots-parser 3.0.1, p-limit 7.3.0, csv-stringify 6.8.1, Playwright 1.61.1, axe-core 4.12.1, original fixtures, loopback servers, emitted-runtime and handle tests, controlled DNS and browser environments, accessibility review, packages, containers, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.
